decisions.title: Commit to GPT-5.1 for Ask SIFT production workloads
decisions.context: ENG-10352 running evals against GPT-5.1. Need to decide if performance/cost justifies migration from current model for Ask SIFT's reasoning engine.

arguments.for: Better reasoning quality improves Signal>Evidence>Decision flow; keeps Ask SIFT competitive; aligns with 'Build the Moat' pillar
arguments.against: Cost implications unclear; evals incomplete; may need multi-model strategy based on query complexity

decisions.title: Prioritize Sift Edge enum/units/descriptions across stack
decisions.context: ENG-10355, 10356, 8857 all adding metadata support to Edge. This is foundational for 'Embed Deeper' - richer semantic layer means better decisions.

arguments.for: Unblocks semantic search in calc channels (ENG-9552); enables richer context for Ask SIFT; improves data quality signals
arguments.against: Migration burden on existing customers; documentation debt (ENG-10220 already behind); edge-to-cloud sync complexity

decisions.title: Standardize channel naming conventions org-wide
decisions.context: ENG-10363 imposing rules on channel names. Signal of data quality issues. Need policy: enforce vs. guide vs. migrate legacy.
decisions.blockedBy: Customer impact assessment incomplete; need migration strategy for existing channels with non-compliant names

arguments.for: Improves searchability (ENG-9552); reduces calc channel bugs (ENG-9807 cache issues); cleaner semantic layer for AI features
arguments.against: Breaking change for existing workflows; Canvas references may break; customer friction during migration

decisions.title: Sunset legacy alert/dashboard config feature flag
decisions.context: ENG-10370 removing FF. Signal that new config system is stable. Need cutover plan and rollback strategy.

arguments.for: Reduces tech debt; simplifies codebase (Nail the Core); frees eng capacity for new features
arguments.against: Risk of regressions in alert delivery; need comprehensive testing across all customer tiers

decisions.title: Invest in histogram/aggregation features vs. other viz
decisions.context: ENG-6409, 8548, 7854, 7855 all histogram/stats work. Families + E2 getting heavy investment. Is this the right viz bet for 2026?

arguments.for: Histograms enable statistical evidence for decisions; quartile/percentile aggregations unlock anomaly detection; families are high-engagement feature
arguments.against: Annotation support (ENG-10213) may deliver more decision-capture value; calc channel UX (searchability) higher ROI for 'Decisions Captured' north star

timeAudit.weekSummary: Heavy collaboration week with 3 customer escalations (calc channel bugs, Edge sync failures). 22% time in unplanned fire-fighting. Q2 planning compressed maker time.
alerts: Only 2 focus blocks today - insufficient for deep technical decisions on GPT-5.1 evals or channel naming policy
3 one-on-ones back-to-back Wed afternoon - no buffer for prep/follow-up
FedRAMP planning meeting rescheduled 2x - risk to Q3 compliance timeline
